metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend depends on seven downstream services. A fault in any of these could propagate to the symptom service (frontend). There is no evidence of other upstream or lateral paths given in the topology, so the plausible propagation path is from any of the listed dependencies to frontend. Without additional cross-service calls among the dependency set, alternative paths are not evidenced by the topology. The blast radius remains limited to the frontend, but monitoring the listed dependencies is critical due to their direct impact on frontend functionality. The topology supports the log agent’s hypothesis about a potential downstream fault being the root cause.

result.synthesis_reasoning: Across sources, there is no observable error signal in logs (Log Agent: no error evidence). Topology identifies multiple direct dependencies of the frontend but provides no definitive single upstream failure; TCB-RCA remains inconclusive with frontend as the sole candidate and no temporal/backtracking evidence to distinguish deeper root causes. Given the absence of concrete log evidence and the presence of plausible propagation paths from several dependencies, we treat all downstream frontend dependencies (cartservice, productcatalogservice, currencyservice) as weak, unobserved root-cause candidates. The final ranking reflects a balanced view: no single confirmed root cause; low confidence due to weak convergence and lack of observable failure signals.
